summary: Linked src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ThrownKirby.c by switching it from NonMatching to Matching, adjusting sdata2 literal symbols to local labels, and adding matching-oriented source changes including local sqrtf/__frsqrte handling, sdata2 ordering anchors, expanded jobj assert/scale access, PAD_STACK/local stack shaping, and a parameterized inlineB2 path for thrown Kirby variants. Review discussion explicitly called parts of the match fake and questioned whether a macro should replace expanded jobj assert code.

key_files.role: Main decomp source updated to link/match ftCo_ThrownKirby functions and supporting inline/helper code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_ThrownKirby.c hunks adding sdata2_order, local sqrtf, expanded jobj assert/scale copy, inlineB2 x18_b0 parameter, PAD_STACK/local changes
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_800BE000 and ftCo_800BDB58 as new 100% matches

key_files.role: Build configuration changed ftCo_ThrownKirby.c from NonMatching to Matching.
sdata2 symbol metadata changed six ftCo_804D8BD0..BF4 global literal symbols into local @212..@217 labels.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: .sdata2 entries at 0x804D8BD0 through 0x804D8BF4 renamed from ftCo_ globals to local @ labels

what_changed.text: Marked ftCo_ThrownKirby.c as Matching in configure.py.
Removed named ftCo_804D8BD0..BF4 literal definitions from the C file and converted corresponding symbols.txt entries to local @212..@217 sdata2 labels.
Added sdata2_order() and a TU-local extern inline sqrtf using __frsqrte, with comments indicating it was moved for linking purposes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_ThrownKirby.c added sdata2_order, extern double __frsqrte, extern inline float sqrtf

what_changed.text: Replaced an HSD_JObjGetScale call in inlineB2 with expanded null checks using __assert("jobj.h", 0x337/0x338, ...) and direct jobj->scale assignment.
Parameterized inlineB2 with bool x18_b0 so ftCo_800BDB58 passes false and ftCo_800BE000 passes true.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineB2 signature and callsite hunks

what_changed.text: Adjusted stack/local layout with PAD_STACK changes and unused u32 locals in ftCo_800BDB58, ftCo_800BE000, ftCo_800BE494, and ftCo_ThrownKirby_Phys.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(36), PAD_STACK(12), unused1/unused2, PAD_STACK(28) hunks

what_changed.text: Bot report recorded two new 100% function matches and linked-code/data increases for GALE01.

smart_moves.text: The PR avoided keeping ordinary literal access through named ftCo_804D8BF4 in one expression, replacing it with inline 1.0f.
The match attempt grouped common thrown-Kirby setup in inlineB2 and exposed the variant x18_b0 behavior as a parameter rather than duplicating the whole setup in the shown diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: inlineB2 bool x18_b0 and callsites

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matching PR relies on local literal-order anchors, TU-local math inlines, expanded asserts, or PAD_STACK/local churn, treat the result as a matching tactic rather than clean authored style unless follow-up evidence proves it is source-shaped.
evidence_refs: PR body: author says improvements could make it neater but could not get them to work
review comment discussion_r3423137868: reviewer calls the sdata2_order/sqrtf hunk a fake match
raw_diff: sdata2_order, local sqrtf, expanded __assert, PAD_STACK changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: For jobj scale access, expanded __assert("jobj.h", ...) blocks may match in an inline context, but review expected a macro/helper check and the author noted __FILE__ complications; preserve this as a cleanup/search target rather than a global style precedent.
evidence_refs: review comment discussion_r3423135179: "isn't there a macro for this?"
review comment discussion_r3423620096: author says resetting __FILE__ would be required because inline is in the wrong file

naming_conventions.text: Temporary/generated names remained in use for uncertain local helpers and fields, including inlineB0/inlineB1/inlineB2, inlineC0, x18_b0, and x18_b1; the PR does not provide semantic evidence to rename them.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: function and field names in ftCo_ThrownKirby.c

naming_conventions.text: sdata2 literals at 0x804D8BD0..0x804D8BF4 were represented as local @ labels rather than named ftCo_804D8BD0..BF4 globals after the PR.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switch object from NonMatching to Matching after source and symbol changes produced new 100% matches for ftCo_800BE000 and ftCo_800BDB58.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists two new matches

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used sdata2_order() with void literal references to influence/order local sdata2 constants.
Moved or redefined sqrtf/__frsqrte locally for linking/matching purposes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed <math_ppc.h>, added extern double __frsqrte and extern inline float sqrtf

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Expanded HSD_JObjGetScale into assert checks plus direct scale assignment, apparently to satisfy inline/file/line behavior.
Adjusted stack layout using PAD_STACK and explicit unused u32 locals.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800BDB58, ftCo_800BE000, ftCo_800BE494, ftCo_ThrownKirby_Phys hunks

review_feedback.author: ThePlayerRolo
review_feedback.comment: Questioned whether a macro existed for the expanded jobj assert/scale access.

review_feedback.author: ThePlayerRolo
review_feedback.comment: Called the sdata2_order/sqrtf linking hunk a fake match and suggested leaving it unlinked.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.comment: Responded that macro cleanup would require resetting __FILE__ because the assert is in the wrong file due to an inline, and considered the comment acceptable.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.comment: Responded to fake-match concern with: "We link fake matches all the time."

follow_up_queries: review fake match leave it unlinked ftCo_ThrownKirby

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR merged, changed file list, review counts
text_corpus: PR body, bot report, review comments
notes: The slice includes diff and comments but no full final file beyond the diff; retained final context outside shown hunks is not proven here.
Bot report provides matching percentages for two functions, but no objdiff/build logs beyond the GitHub bot summary are included.
Review discussion documents disagreement about fake matches; intake does not infer broader project acceptance from one merged PR.
Several changes conflict with current loaded standards as cleanup candidates, but the historical PR evidence only proves they were used here, not that they are accepted style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR2711 used explicit matching tactics to link ftCo_ThrownKirby, with reviewer-visible fake-match concerns
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2711, ftCo_ThrownKirby.c was linked by changing configure.py to Matching and using tactics including sdata2_order literal references, local sqrtf/__frsqrte code, expanded jobj asserts, and PAD_STACK/local layout changes. Review called the sdata2_order/sqrtf hunk a fake match and questioned macro cleanup for expanded jobj asserts. Treat this PR as evidence of a historical matching compromise and as a cleanup search target, not as evidence that these shapes are preferred authored style.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR2711 as an example for matching tactics needing evidence and cleanup notes
source_update_candidates.text: PR2711 is a concrete historical example where sdata2 ordering anchors, local math inline relocation, expanded header asserts, PAD_STACK, and unused locals were used to link a file, while review called part of it a fake match. Curator may attach it as an example under matching-tactics-need-evidence/header-inlines/literals standards, framed as a caution rather than an accepted source shape.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR directly demonstrates tactic-heavy matching and reviewer concern, but should not be promoted as a preferred standard.

source_update_candidates.title: ftCo_ThrownKirby.c has historical fake-match cleanup candidates around sdata2_order/sqrtf and jobj inline asserts
source_update_candidates.text: Historical PR2711 linked ftCo_ThrownKirby.c using sdata2_order, local sqrtf/__frsqrte, expanded jobj.h asserts, and stack-shaping edits. Review comments identified fake-match and macro/helper concerns. Future work in this file should search these areas before treating the shapes as authored.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path-scoped historical context may help future decomp cleanup, but requires curator review against current source.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that fake matches are globally accepted project style from the comment "We link fake matches all the time"; it is a single review-thread response in this PR.

Do not promote TU-local sqrtf/__frsqrte relocation, sdata2_order anchors, expanded __assert blocks, unused locals, or PAD_STACK changes as accepted decomp standards without independent build/objdiff and style evidence.
Do not infer semantic names for inlineB0/inlineB1/inlineB2 or x18_b0/x18_b1 from this PR; the slice provides no semantic naming proof.
